items.q: What are typical units for the Hubble constant?
items.correct: km per second per megaparsec (km/s/Mpc) — recession speed per unit distance.
items.misconception: Metres per second — it's the speed at which the universe expands.
items.random_wrong: Light-years — it's a measure of cosmic distance.
items.why: Students treat H0 as a speed or distance rather than a rate of expansion (speed per unit distance), missing the structure of Hubble's law.

items.q: Does Hooke's law apply to a spring for any amount of stretching?
items.correct: No — it only holds up to the limit of proportionality; beyond that the relationship between force and extension is no longer linear.
items.misconception: Yes — Hooke's law applies to any spring at any extension.
items.random_wrong: Only when the force is small enough to be ignored.
items.why: Students apply F = kx universally without noting the limit of proportionality, leading to over-prediction at large extensions.

items.q: What does the cosmological redshift of distant galaxies indicate?
items.correct: Space itself is expanding, stretching the wavelength of light from distant galaxies as it travels.
items.misconception: Galaxies are moving through space away from us, like objects flying outward from a centre.
items.random_wrong: The galaxies are turning red because they are getting older.
items.why: Students apply the Doppler effect literally; the expansion of space is a more subtle picture they often miss.
The answer examiners want is: 8.31 J/K/mol. “0.0821” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Mean translational KE of ideal gas molecule equals:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: (3/2)kT. “(1/2)kT” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Mean KE of an ideal gas molecule at 300 K (in × 10⁻²¹ J):
items.why: The real figure is 6.2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
